Acme's trailing-12 TX sales look close to the threshold, and I want a second look on whether we stay origin-based/no filing versus move to local per-ZIP sourcing.\\n\\nIf TX nexus is triggered, I'll rerun the state using local rates and update the quarter total here.\", \"user\": \"persona_027\", \"reply_count\": 3, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1777389480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_0\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

structuredContent.result:
[{"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0003", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

I reviewed the two proposed scope lines and agree they are clean expansions of the FY2026 outsourced-services grid under the existing Acme Cloud engagement. I do not see any audit-side independence concerns with adding the multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC or the AR-aging bucket cleanup as drafted.

Once Andrea's revisions are incorporated into the addendum language and the client countersignature is back, I am comfortable countersigning Engagement Letter Addendum #1 on the firm side.

This should give you the approval chain you need before filing.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": ["andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T19:48:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "ad391950add941e1bdd66f3754e64a55", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "nia.okafor@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "sean.williams@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Mon 3/2 onboarding: assign owners and flag document blockers", "content": "Hi George,

Following up on Monday's kickoff, I agree with the split between banking items and general support documents. On the bookkeeping side, I am blocked until I receive the prior balance sheet support, chart of accounts history, and any sales tax or payroll registrations. Once those are in hand, I can finish my review and confirm what is ready to go out versus what still needs client follow-up.

Best,
Nia", "cc": [], "parent_id": "dc9fe1ec878c41a58dc73ba627ad9003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-02T16:01:29Z", "is_read": true}]

content.text: May has no sales-tax JE yet — confirming nobody started May. Let me extract the full April reclass JE (lines + memos) to learn the per-state structure and basis.

input.command:
f=/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/8ba13136-c47f-463e-b61e-d81acd6023ea/tool-results/mcp-mcp_f94a35f7-oracle_gl_list_journal_entries-1781602943317.txt
# find the JE id near the sales tax description
python3 - "$f" <<'EOF'
import sys,json,re
raw=open(sys.argv[1]).read()
# the file is {"result":"<escaped json>"}
obj=json.loads(raw)
data=json.loads(obj["result"])
jes=data.get("journal_entries") or data.get("entries") or []
print("total JEs:", len(jes))
for je in jes:
    blob=json.dumps(je).lower()
    if "sales tax" in blob:
        print("=====", je.get("id"), je.get("status"), je.get("entry_type"))
        print("desc:", je.get("description"))
        print("bj:", je.get("business_justification"))
        for ln in je.get("lines",[]):
            print("  ", ln.get("account_number"), ln.get("debit"), ln.get("credit"), "| memo:", ln.get("memo"))
EOF

The GL trial balance is placeholder data with no state dimension, so May must be staged on the April basis and trued to the final May cut before Ming's sign-off — I won't fabricate precision or post any JE before approval. input.command:
cat > /tmp/may_packet.txt <<'EOF'
BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS - INTERNAL / RESTRICTED
May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet  (DRAFT - FOR APPROVAL)

Entity:        Brookfield CPAs & Advisors (firm's own books)
Period:        brookfield_FP-2026-05 (May 2026)
Prepared by:   Hannah Grant, Corporate Tax Senior - 2026-06-16
Reviewer:      Sofia Halabi
Approver:      Ming Chang  (partner sign-off REQUIRED before any return is filed or payment scheduled)
Jurisdictions: New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, Pennsylvania
Deadlines:     NY / NJ / CT due June 22, 2026 ;  PA due June 25, 2026

------------------------------------------------------------------
1. WHY THIS CYCLE IS OPENING LATE
------------------------------------------------------------------
The recurring monthly kickoff tickler (due 2026-06-01) was missed, so May prep had not
been started by anyone. This packet opens and stages the May cycle. The returns are due
the back half of June, so the cycle is not yet at risk, but it is now in motion and routed
for approval rather than left untouched.

------------------------------------------------------------------
2. PRELIMINARY LIABILITY BY STATE
   (carried on the April basis; to be trued to the final May taxable-services
    cut from brookfield_FP-2026-05 before filing)
------------------------------------------------------------------
State | Basis                              | Rate                | May (prelim.) | April (approved, comparative)
NY    | Taxable advisory/consulting svcs   | as filed            |   ~ $12,400   |   ~ $12,400
NJ    | Taxable services                   | as filed            |   see note    |   } combined
CT    | Taxable services                   | as filed            |   see note    |   }  ~ $11,050
PA    | Taxable services                   | 6% (econ. nexus)    |     $3,950    |     $3,950
                                                                  ----------------------------------
PRELIMINARY MAY TOTAL                                              |   ~ $27,400   |   $27,400 (approved)

Notes:
 - NY: April stepped up ~18% MoM because two new advisory engagements went live mid-April.
   Those engagements continue into May, so NY is carried at the April run-rate (~$12,400)
   pending the May cut rather than assuming a further increase.
 - NJ & CT: trailing-three-month-average states; carried at the April basis (~$11,050
   combined). The individual NJ/CT split is to be confirmed from the May cut.
 - PA: see Section 3.

------------------------------------------------------------------
3. PENNSYLVANIA - TREATMENT AND OPEN FLAGS  (please read before approving)
------------------------------------------------------------------
Where PA has landed since we registered:
 - March 2026: Brookfield crossed PA's $100,000 economic-nexus threshold.
 - April 2026: first filing month under the new threshold. Treatment applied was 6% PA
   state rate on PA taxable services = $3,950 (implied taxable base ~ $65,833). Reviewed by
   Sofia Halabi and approved by Ming Chang inside the $27,400 April packet; filed in the
   May 20 batch (PA statutorily due May 25).
 - This April cycle is the ONLY post-registration precedent.

Proposed May treatment: identical basis - 6% on PA taxable services, carried at $3,950
pending the May cut, i.e. consistent with where April landed.

Open items flagged for your decision (NOT assumed through):
 a) Single data point. PA has exactly one prior cycle since registration, so "consistent
    treatment" rests on April alone. Please confirm 6%-state-on-taxable-services is the
    standing method going forward.
 b) Local rate. No PA local add-on (Philadelphia +2% / Allegheny +1%) was applied in April.
    That is correct only if our PA-sourced taxable services are not sourced to those
    localities. Confirm the May client/sourcing mix is unchanged.
 c) Filing frequency. Confirm PA's assigned cadence for our new registration includes a
    May monthly return (newly registered filers are sometimes set to a different frequency).
 d) Final basis. The May PA taxable base must be confirmed from the FP-2026-05 cut before
    filing (the period still shows open).

------------------------------------------------------------------
4. ROUTING / NEXT STEPS
------------------------------------------------------------------
 - Approval: this packet requires Ming Chang's sign-off before any return is filed or any
   payment is scheduled. Sofia Halabi to review per the April process.
 - On approval: true the per-state figures to the final May taxable-services cut, book the
   May sales-tax liability the same way April was booked (reclass across 240000 / 230000)
   before the FP-2026-05 lock, then file NY/NJ/CT by June 22 and PA by June 25.
 - No journal entry has been posted and no return has been filed at this stage - the cycle
   is staged only and is awaiting approval.

------------------------------------------------------------------
5. TRAIL
------------------------------------------------------------------
 - Filed alongside the April packet: Records Vault doc_1af8dfb919a9444d
   "Brookfield April 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet".
 - Source/precedent: April kickoff thread in #tax-prep-and-filings; April reclass JE
   je_5b8169fa03b04149 ($27,400, NY/NJ/CT/PA); recurring kickoff tickler scen_045.
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/may_packet.txt > /tmp/may_packet.b64
echo "bytes:"; wc -c < /tmp/may_packet.txt; echo "b64 chars:"; wc -c < /tmp/may_packet.b64

input.subject: For approval: Brookfield May 2026 sales-tax packet (NY/NJ/CT/PA) - PA flag inside
input.content:
Hi Ming,

I've opened and staged the May 2026 sales-tax cycle for our own books and am routing it to you for sign-off before anything is filed - the approval on these is yours, not ours. Nothing has been filed and no JE has been posted yet.

Packet: Records Vault doc_af3c2c9e7400 "Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet" - filed alongside the April packet (doc_1af8dfb919a9444d).

Background: the monthly kickoff tickler (due 6/1) was missed, so May hadn't been started. We're not in crisis - NY/NJ/CT are due June 22 and PA June 25 - but I wanted it moving and set up properly rather than left to become a scramble.

Preliminary liability (carried on the April basis, to be trued to the final May taxable-services cut before filing):
 - NY ~ $12,400 (April run-rate; the two new advisory engagements that stepped NY up mid-April continue into May)
 - NJ + CT ~ $11,050 combined (trailing-average states, April basis; individual split to confirm from the May cut)
 - PA $3,950 (6% on taxable services - post-economic-nexus, consistent with April)
 - Preliminary total ~ $27,400 (in line with the approved April packet)

Pennsylvania - flagging rather than assuming it through, since you asked us to keep this consistent with where PA has landed since we registered:
 - April was our first and ONLY filing since crossing PA's $100K economic-nexus threshold in March, applied at 6% state rate on taxable services ($3,950). May mirrors that.
 - Open items for your call: (a) the 6%-state-on-taxable-services method rests on that single April cycle - please confirm it's the standing basis; (b) no PA local add-on (Philadelphia/Allegheny) was applied in April - fine only if our PA sourcing is unchanged; (c) confirm PA's assigned filing frequency includes a May monthly return; (d) final PA base to be confirmed from the FP-2026-05 cut.

Once you've approved (and Sofia's had her review pass per the April process), I'll true the figures to the May cut, book the liability the way April was booked, and file NY/NJ/CT by 6/22 and PA by 6/25.

Thanks,
Hannah

I've staged the packet in Records Vault (doc_af3c2c9e7400 "Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet"), alongside April's, and routed it to Ming for approval (Sofia cc'd for review). Preliminary total ~$27,400, carried on the April basis pending the final May taxable-services cut. PA is held at the 6% post-economic-nexus rate consistent with April, with a few open points flagged in the packet for Ming. Nothing filed and no JE posted until it's approved. Will update once it's signed off and the figures are trued to the May cut.

content.text:
The May cycle is now open, staged, and routed. Here's where it stands.

## What I found
- **Nobody had started May.** The monthly kickoff tickler (due 6/1) fired but was never picked up; there's no May sales-tax JE in `brookfield_FP-2026-05` and no May packet existed. Returns are due **NY/NJ/CT June 22, PA June 25** — late start, not yet at risk.
- **April is the reference point.** April was a single $27,400 reclass (JE `je_5b8169fa03b04149`) covering NY/NJ/CT/PA, approved by Ming. The packet lives at Records Vault **doc_1af8dfb919a9444d**.
- **PA's only post-registration precedent is April.** Brookfield crossed PA's $100K economic-nexus threshold in March; April was the first filing, at **6% on taxable services = $3,950**.

## On Pennsylvania (flagged, not assumed)
I held PA at the **6% post-economic-nexus rate, $3,950**, consistent with April — the basis the recurring reminder explicitly tells us to keep. But because that rests on a *single* prior cycle, I did **not** quietly carry it through; I flagged four open points for Ming: (a) confirm 6%-on-taxable-services is the standing method, (b) no local PA add-on (Philadelphia/Allegheny) was applied — fine only if sourcing is unchanged, (c) confirm the assigned filing frequency includes a May monthly return, (d) final base to be trued to the FP-2026-05 cut.

A note on the figures: the GL revenue isn't split by state and the trial balance returns as placeholder/unbalanced data, so there's no clean May per-state cut to pull yet. Rather than fabricate precision, I staged the per-state schedule on the April basis (NY ~$12,400; NJ+CT ~$11,050 combined; PA $3,950; ~$27,400 total) explicitly marked **preliminary, to be trued to the final May cut before filing**. I posted no JE and filed nothing — that's Ming's sign-off to give.

## What I did
1. **Staged the packet** — "Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet" (**doc_af3c2c9e7400**), same kind/classification/retention as April's, filed alongside it so the trail stays in one place.
2. **Routed to Ming for approval** (email, Sofia cc'd as reviewer per the April process) with the PA flags up top.
3. **Notified the tax team** in #tax-prep-and-filings that May is finally moving.
4. **Set a dated reminder** on your list for **June 19** covering the NY/NJ/CT (6/22) and PA (6/25) deadlines, so the filing dates don't slip the way the kickoff did.

Next step is Ming's sign-off; once it's in, true the figures to the May cut, book the liability the way April was booked, and file.
